title: gouldia fosbergii
intro: Gouldia fosbergii, also known as the Hawaiian sandalwood, is a rare and endangered tree species endemic to the island of Kauai in Hawaii. This plant is valued for its timber and essential oil, which has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The essential oil extracted from Gouldia fosbergii has been reported to exhibit health benefits such as reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and improving respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including essential oil, tincture, and salve, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
